The heart of the classic nike rotation. This is a really good Pegasus. I’ve ran in versions 37, 38, 39, 40, and now 41, and I have to say this is the best one yet for what it is - a mileage workhorse. This is not the fastest shoe in the market, not the most cushioned shoe in the market, nor the most supportive. However, it is responsive, relatively lightweight, and stable enough to allow you to show up and log mile after mile after mile without it feeling like a chore. Especially with the new wider toe box, this really is now the shoe for those 30 to 90 minute every day runs that allow you to build your fitness from the ground up day after day. Perfect for easy runs with strides, medium long runs, and maybe a little bit of uptempo if you’re feeling good that day. Also great for doing warmups, drills, cooldowns, and light gym work.  The only downside with this shoe is that, compared to Pegasus of the past, working out in this shoe does leave a lot to be desired. But then again, it got a lot better at long runs and recovery runs, which suggests that this really is that mileage shoe with a classic feeling for every day runs when you don’t want to overdo it.  If you don’t like ground feel and you prefer a more cruise-y / rockered ride, then I would look at the Vomero instead. And if you need a faster shoe, I would look at the Zoom Fly, Vaporfly, or Alphafly depending on your budget and your needs

Beware Metatarsal Support!. I fell in love with this shoe the moment I put it on but the love affair didn’t list.   Pros: This shoe is nearly perfect for someone with a mostly straight, extra wide foot. The toebox doesn’t taper too much and squish the square shaped footed of us. And it doesn’t make the heavier of us sink into too much.   Cons: it has built in metatarsal support. Great everyday trainer from a classic model!. I’ve been running for most of my life and have ran in many different Pegasus models. Most recently, I went through four pairs of the 40 in my marathon training last year and this year. I really like the 41 though. I like that the Nike symbol is a little smaller and filled in solidly this time around. The overall look reminds of some of the 90s air maxes (without the airbag of course). The ride is smooth and the fit is what I expect. At this point, the Pegasus is a fastball down the middle. I generally know what I’m getting. It doesn’t offer some of the high end features of the Invincible, but this a great everyday neutral cushion trainer that you can really stack miles on. I’d argue you can get more mileage out of these than Althea Invincible, so if just depends what you prefer. After not loving the look for a bit, the 41 is great and a breath of fresh air, which makes me happy. Going from the Pegasus in training runs to the Valorfly’s on race day has been a great combination for me and I look forward to many more runs in the Pegasus 41!

True Love. I adore this iteration of the Pegasus! I was a Pegasus wearer for years, but abandoned them around 2018 to try “better” shoes. I returned to the Peg this year because I just could not find a daily trainer I loved, and I am so glad I gave the Pegasus another chance.   I was so excited about them out of the box on my easy 7 mile run that I chose them for my 20 miler over the weekend, and they delivered. Excellent cushioning while still feeling light and flexible underfoot. I ran on a mix of pavement, gravel, and dirt trail, and they had good traction and stability without having that over-built feeling so many shoes have now.   The upper is super comfy. I do have a narrow foot, so for me, the fit was perfect, but it is a narrower shoe with a sock-like fit. It runs true to size in terms of length.   If you like a super built up shoe with a ginormous amount of forefoot cushion, this may not be your jam. But if you love a shoe that allows your foot to move naturally without too much bulk underfoot, i would highly recommend. I have five other brands in my rotation right now, and this is easily my favorite trainer of the past couple years

Uncomfortable and Disappointing. I recently purchased a pair of Nike shoes with high expectations, given the brand's reputation for quality athletic footwear. Unfortunately, my experience with these particular shoes has been extremely disappointing.  Firstly, the material used in the construction of these shoes feels cheap and uncomfortable. It lacks the flexibility and breathability that I usually associate with Nike products. The synthetic upper feels stiff and unforgiving, leading to discomfort during prolonged wear.  Moreover, the cushioning and support provided by the shoes are subpar. I expected a more comfortable and responsive feel, especially considering the premium price tag associated with the Nike brand. The insole seems inadequate, providing minimal shock absorption and leaving my feet fatigued after even short periods of use.  Durability is another significant concern. Despite the prominent Nike logo and branding, the overall build quality of these shoes does not live up to the standards I've come to expect. After just a few weeks of use, signs of wear and tear started to appear, making me question the longevity of the product.  Additionally, the design, while visually appealing, seems to prioritize aesthetics over functionality. The narrow fit and lack of proper arch support make these shoes unsuitable for those with wider feet or individuals seeking enhanced comfort for extended activities.  In conclusion, these Nike shoes have been a regrettable purchase. The uncomfortable material, lack of adequate support, and questionable durability make them a poor choice for anyone seeking a reliable and comfortable pair of athletic shoes. I would strongly advise potential buyers to consider alternative options within the same price range that offer better quality and performance

Lighten up on the upper. First some positives. The Vomero looks fantastic. When this shoe has reached the end of its running life, it will fit in great as a street wear shoe, which I can't say for many of my running shoes. It's well cushioned, but you already knew that. It's also very stable. And the waffle tread on the outsole provides fantastic grip. The downsides are all related. The Vomero is extremely plush, heavy, and warm. First the plush - the upper is downright pillowy. When you lace up, the tongue just spills out, swallowing the laces like a pillow. It's really impossible to overstate how thick and plush the tongue and the rest of the upper is on this shoe. All that pillowy softness adds weight, which is noticeable, especially on long runs. And, as you might expect, breathability is terrible. There's essentially no airflow and your feet will feel really warm unless it's cold outside. I also own a pair of Nike Invincible 3's. The run is just as cushioned (and a bit unstable) with a massive slab of ZoomX foam underfoot. But the upper on the Invincibles is engineered for breathability and weight savings. I don't mind a heavy shoe where the weight is due to the foam underfoot. For me, though, the weight isn't worth it if that weight comes from a plush upper - especially with the problems of breathability. Switching to an engineered, lightweight upper would be a massive improvement. As-is, the Vomero is hard to recommend

Effortless Miles, Unmatched Comfort. The Nike Vomero 18 has completely levelled up my long-distance game. True to size with a plush ride that feels like I’m floating over the road. Easily the best upgrade this season. Highly recommend for anyone chasing comfort, distance, and performance in one slick package

Amazing. I have the Pegasus 38, 39, and 41 (one really dirty, one for the gym, and one for outdoor runs), plus the Motiva (for work and free time). So yeah… I didn’t really need another pair of shoes. 😅 But after reading the reviews, I figured I might as well try them on in the store. I also tested some of the top-rated shoes from other brands.  And color me surprised—the Vomero 18 is incredibly soft, yet still has a bit of bounce. The toe box is super spacious, and despite how soft it feels, the shoe still offers decent stability. I’ll have to see how well it holds up over time, but as long as it doesn’t go stiff or fall apart, I think I’ve found a new favorite work/free time shoe.  As mentioned, I tried a few competitors as well. Some came pretty soft as well, but they were often more expensive and often lacked that nice bounce that I found in Vomero 18

Cushioning beyond belief. Wow, I am completely blown away by how cushioned this shoe is. Went out for my first run this year and was pain free and it held up quite well - no drama - nothing...was a solid cushion on the feet!! Dont take my word, try a pair out yourself and see!
Amazing!!!. I suffer from plantar fasciitis on my right heel, and let me tell you I had no hope until I put these bad boys on my feet. Comfort is amazing !!!

VOMERO EXPLODE!. As a marathon runner who wears Nike elite racing shoes that are notoriously restrictive, this new Vomero is a welcome surprise. The fit is very generous and ample and the cushion is beyond comfortable. If you think it’s too big just tie it tighter. Suggestion Nike: Create a low price racing Vomero with a curved carbon fiber or nylon insert and watch the revenue explode. In fact call it the VOMERO EXPLODE! This shoe is simple enough to help anyone train without strain and be ready for the big race, but it may also keep competitors up at night!

Worth the Money!. I’ve only been wearing these a few days, but so far they are the most amazing shoes. I have peripheral neuropathy and my feet hurt a lot. These are like walking on clouds. Puts a little bounce in my step, literally, but my feet have very little pain when I wear these and do not fatigue much at all. I highly recommend these shoes! And will be purchasing more!

Max Cushioning for real. They really do mean max cushioning wow. This is my first pair of Nike running shoes and yeah wow they make a big difference. i’m a heavier set runner and i often experience knee pain - i didn’t get that at all with these shoes. My first run with them was entirely knee pain free. i felt supported and cushioned on every stride . i don’t run for performance or speed, at the moment i just do it as a fun way to keep fit. If you’re looking for max speed look elsewhere, if you want comfort and support during your long runs, these are defo the shoe for you. I also don’t feel like they’re too heavy which is the main issue i see with other reviewers. they’re also not too hot. Honestly to me they do exactly what they advertise and they do it well. Vomero 18s gained a new fan. i also love the red orange and pink sunset design. just a fantastic all-round shoe!

Great cushioning and style. Very soft and nice feeling to the cushioning. I also have the Brooks Glycerin Max which is super cushioned, but I’d say those are slightly firmer. The Vomero 18’s feel “slightly” less stable, I assume due to the softness, but not bad. Kinda similar to the NB 1080v14.  For some reason the outside of my left foot was a little achy while running but they may just need to break in more. Overall, I would definitely recommend. Hopefully they come out with more colors. I waited until the all black came out since I don’t like white sneakers that much

Nothing better exists. If I could give 10 stars I would. This is the most comfortable shoe I’ve ever put on my foot. I’ve owned many other brands and none have compared to these. Very comfortable, I love my Shoe

Amazing Comfort and Performance – Nike Vomero Delivers!. I recently got a pair of Nike Vomero running shoes, and they have exceeded my expectations! From the moment I put them on, I could feel the plush cushioning and support. The ZoomX foam provides an incredibly soft yet responsive ride, making long runs feel effortless.  The fit is perfect—snug but not too tight—offering great stability without restricting movement. The breathable upper keeps my feet cool, and the traction is excellent, even on wet pavement. Whether for daily training or long-distance running, the Vomero is a game-changer.  If you’re looking for a shoe that combines comfort, durability, and top-tier performance, I highly recommend the Nike Vomero!

BEYOND AMAZING. There are literally no words, but I’ll try! I’m not a runner, so I just got these to walk around in. Super comfortable. Super comfortable, wore them to the gym today. True to size, no pinching on the sides of the foot. Decent. Squeak / weight. So close to being excellent. Part of my review is practical and part is personal preference -  Practical: squeaks. The shoe squeaks. It’s one of my greatest pet peeves and worries and frustrations with “air” - it doesn’t always happen, but when it does, it’s horrible.  I don’t like noisy or squeaky shoes of any kind - and air can be particularly bad.  In these, only the right shoe squeaks. And it’s every step. A two-beat squeak. It’s awful.  To me, it speaks to the fact that it doesn’t have to be that way (one shoe doesn’t do it) and therefore somewhere the quality was lower in the right shoe.  Usually, with air, it takes a puncture or etc for stuff like that to happen / over time. This immediate squeak just made me sad.   Personal preference: I don’t like a bulky shoe. I prefer slim and athletic - I think these are less bulky than I imagined (and I’m definitely not saying “heavy”) but they’re…thick. And naturally, the midsole and outsole combined are weightier than the upper. It’s difficult for this not to happen in making a shoe, but I don’t want to feel as if I’m lifting / dragging some weight off the ground as the upper holds on tight to it. I want the shoe to feel like one “whole.” I come at this from a soccer players perspective. The footwear should be connected as one. Disconnected is not a good feeling.  Now, runners who run run run a lot may find it to work out well over long distances / many miles. I don’t imagine that being the case, but it may be.   Air has the benefit of not ever really losing the bounce - as foam can compress eventually and then it’s done - but air adds weight and variables such as squeaks - and height which can lead to some roll/slightly less stability (not calling the shoe unstable) -  And I personally don’t love that feeling.   I digress - tho I’m ready to work in Nike design and testing whenever they’re ready to have me :!

Squeaky Nightmare – Beware of These Shoes!. I had high hopes for these shoes, but unfortunately, they turned out to be one of the most annoying pairs I’ve ever owned. Right out of the box, they looked great—sleek design, comfortable fit—but the moment I started walking, the nightmare began.  These shoes squeak with every single step, no matter what surface I’m on. Tile? Squeak. Hardwood? Squeak. Even on carpet, there’s still a faint noise. It’s like having a built-in sound effect following me everywhere. I’ve tried everything—adjusting the laces, checking the insoles, even applying powder to the soles—but nothing stops the relentless noise.  What’s worse, the squeaking isn’t just a minor annoyance—it’s LOUD. I wore them to work once, and people kept turning to see where the sound was coming from. Walking through a quiet hallway or a library? Forget it. You might as well announce your arrival from a mile away.  Comfort-wise, they’re decent, but honestly, that doesn’t matter when I feel like I’m starring in my own personal sound effects reel. Unless you enjoy the embarrassment of sounding like a human rubber duck, I strongly recommend avoiding these shoes

Awesome recovery shoe.. I have really enjoyed running in the Nike Vomero Plus. The cushioning is the standout feature. It feels plush and protective without being unstable, which makes a big difference on longer runs and recovery days. My legs feel noticeably fresher after higher mileage sessions.  The ride is smooth and consistent, especially at easy to steady paces. Heel to toe transitions feel controlled, and the shoe gives me confidence on pavement when I am settling into a rhythm. The upper is comfortable and breathable, with enough room in the forefoot to stay comfortable as miles add up.  This is not a speed focused shoe, and it does feel heavier when I try to push the pace. For tempo runs or faster efforts, I would reach for something lighter. But for daily training, long runs, and steady aerobic work, it does exactly what I want.  Overall, the Vomero Plus is a dependable, comfort driven running shoe that excels at protecting my legs and keeping runs enjoyable. It is a great choice for runners who value cushioning, stability, and consistency over pure speed

Great Max Cushion Shoe. Preparing for a marathon and needed a new pair of shoes for my long easy runs. I was a BIG fan of the ZoomX Invincible 1, a slightly lesser fan of the ZI2, and didn’t like the ZI3 at all (not a bad shoe, just wasn’t for me). I switched to Brooks for my last marathon and they didn’t hold up well, which put me back in the market. When I read about the Vomero Plus picking up where the Invincible line left off, I was intrigued. I have to say, 126 miles later, this is an awesome max cushion shoe. Combines the best elements of the Invincible line with key improvements, particularly the upper and the outsole. Also true to size (whereas I used to order 1/2 size up). My legs and feet feel incredible after long slow runs and even picking up speed due to the surprising energy return for a max cushion. I am definitely buying another pair when these retire, hopefully around 300 miles. FYI I rotate the Zoom Fly 6 for shorter, faster runs and it seems to be a good combination. I’m not a competitive runner so I didn’t need to buy the more expensive models

Finished 1st 10K Run!. I'm 5'10, around 197 pounds, and I'm in my forties. And I have been training as a runner for a couple of years now. For most of this year, I have been using the Pegasus 41 for daily runs, speed runs, and 5K runs. I also have the Vaporfly 3, which I use exclusively in organized racing. While I am pleased with those shoes in most situations, I felt like I needed a shoe that will help me run longer and farther. And I don't want to wear out the Vaporflys too early. The Vomero Plus is the shoe that helps me in those regards. I was able to have an uninterrupted run for about 80 minutes. And I was able to run for 10,000 meters for the first time. Mind you, the shoe doesn't feel particularly fast. However, it took longer for my feet to get tired while wearing the Vomero Plus, thanks to the generous amount of ZoomX cushioning. I'm quite sure that I had more fun while wearing the Vomero Plus. I just threw on my headphones and ran while listening to a 60-minute podcast episode. Also, this shoe is a little wider in the midfoot than the Peg 41. At the same time, my heel felt pretty stable. And I appreciate how there is more rubber on the outsole than the Peg 41, so the grip is nice. I plan on rotating all three shoes for different goals. But as of today, the Vomero Plus just could be one of my favorite running shoes of all time!

Return to form!. Writing this review after 6 weeks and 75 miles.  First the good.  This is a fantastic training shoe.  I've taken these out mostly for long/easy runs between five and 14 miles.  The cushioning is superb, I've had no stability issues, and only one hot spot that never occurred again so probably a sock problem.  They also cope with picking up the pace, and respond well to tempo/half marathon pace intervals. Fit true to size, with good room in the toe box.  And the bad.  I've taken a star off as the sole is already showing signs of serious wear on the heel after only 75 miles running on flat pavement/road, so don't have much hope of getting to 250.  Which is a real shame, because these have become my go to easy/recovery shoe after eight years of Asics Nimbus.  Same again for the Plus 2 next year with a more durable sole, please Nike!

Comfortable shoe to build miles during the week and long runs. I’ve run about 90 miles in these over the last 18 days and I would describe the difference in feel, compared to running in Nike Frees (which I also love) being like going from sleeping on a pad on the floor to sleeping on a memory foam mattress. I’m increasing mileage now as I build up for my next marathon and also wanted something that would help alleviate tension around both Achilles and switching to these shoes seems to help with that. These feel like they are going to help run a ton of miles without wearing down. They do have a little bit of a bounce/wobble to them, but I ultimately like this. I also like it that they don’t have a flyplate, it’s great to have this cushioning without that same amount of forward propulsion for training. One thing to note, the offset is 10mm and I normally run in 8mm or less. I was hesitant to get something with a greater heel to toe drop because I tried that once and it felt like running with a door jam under foot, like blocking my heel from dropping normally. Thankfully, these squish enough when you land that the extra 2mm compared to what I’m used to is not noticeable at all. Finally, while I like these a lot, I would be very curious to try these with more of a Flyknit upper because the upper does feel like a bit of extra cushion and it takes some attention to get the laces tied up tight so that they don’t feel too big or loose. I just saw the overview of the Vomero Premium before I wrote this, and it looks like the new option is a little bit trimmed down in the upper, which looks like it will be great

Plush Cushioning with Surprising Stability – Great for Long Miles. I picked up the Vomero Plus for daily road runs, and they’ve quickly become my go‑to. The fit runs true to size in my regular width, and even in the extra‑wide option, the lockdown feels secure without pinching. Despite the tall cushioning stack, they feel stable — I never got the “wobbly” sensation I’ve had with other max‑cushion shoes. The ZoomX midsole is the star here — ultra‑soft underfoot yet noticeably springy, which makes longer runs and recovery days a lot easier on my legs. The engineered mesh upper breathes well and wraps the foot comfortably, while the padding around the collar adds a premium feel. Style‑wise, the bold stack gives a chunkier profile, so if you prefer a super‑sleek look, this may not be your pick, but I like the mix of performance and standout design. At $180, it’s an investment, but the comfort, durability, and versatile ride make it a solid value if you log lots of miles

After having gone without a pair of everyday shoes long enough, absolutely no doubt about it, it was time for me to get another pair of shoes. Upon purchasing my very first pair of Nike Air Force 1, a classic, all-white (low), I had finally realized all of the reasons that this sneaker is still one of the most popular shoes available today. This review will evaluate the AF1 of its Design, Comfort, Durability, Versatility and Overall Value so that I can provide an honest opinion on how these shoes are for everyday wear. The Air Force 1 was the first ever basketball shoe to feature Nike Air technology, revolutionizing the game and sneaker culture forever. Over three decades since its first release, the Air Force 1 remains true to its roots while earning its status as a fashion staple for seasons to come. I ordered the shoes straight from the Nike app and they had come in surprisingly quick. The design of the box wasn't very special, but it was classic and stood out. The first look of the Air Force 1 has a pure white look that grabs your attention. The shoe is smooth leather and has subtle Nike swoosh. The all white look gives that sense of cleanliness of the shoes. The shoe cuts just below the ankle gives a good shape that works well with many of my outfits. What I like most is how the shoe is stylish without being super showy. It’s a shoe I have been able to wear in school, going out, or even more dressed-up events.  My first experience with putting on my pair of AF1s was that they were way more comfortable than I expected. I had heard a lot of reviews about this shoe from people before I bought them, I believe they are a pretty comfortable shoe. There is definitely a lot of cushioning under your foot that is soft while you walk in the shoe. Although the shoe is a little heavier than your typical sneaker. If you haven't ever put your foot through a new pair of shoes for several hours, you will discover that just after sitting for a while, they will start to feel just a little bit weird, but after about a full day of wearing them, they will start to fit naturally on your feet and will not cause any issues when worn for long periods of time. The comfort of the shoe is essential to everyday sneaker wear. If you plan to be wearing AF1s regularly, you will certainly enjoy the comfort. One of the reasons I think the cost is justified is because of the durability of these shoes. The leather resists quick wear-and-tear and the creases do develop at the top of the shoes. The creases can be helped by buying a crease protection which I had to do. After wearing my shoes for about a month I have still been able to keep them clean. The laces are pretty easy to keep clean but dust and dirt are a great attraction to them.  Overall, the Nike Air Force 1 has definitely earned a place in my regular rotation of shoes. I am definitely glad I decided to finally get AF1's. Its classic design goes great with a lot of my outfits. They also have been a shoe I can use for nice events or even just dinners.

I’m able to walk for the first time in over two years. I asked God for healing and He gave me a pair of shoes, Nike Motiva. I broke every ankle bone and suffered severe dislocation in my right foot, May 2023. They inserted a 12 inch nail from the bottom of my heel to the top of my tibia. I had severe pain with the nail, so they removed it 2 days before Thanksgiving, 2024. I just healed from the incisions and bruising a couple of months ago. I live on a fixed income, so I couldn’t buy shoes until my birthday in May. I had one pair of shoes, in which I only used the left shoe with a walking boot. I bought a pair of Women’s Nike Motiva. I normally wear an 8.5. The website suggested a half size larger, since Nike runs small. I chose a 9.5, since my toes need more room and I need to account for a larger right foot with the bone calcification. I put them on and stood up to take my first step in shoes, in over two years. I don’t have a right calf muscle, due to muscle atrophy, so I have to walk carefully. I could not believe it. I was walking on a CUSHION in shoes for the first time since my ankle break. I don’t have a limp, weird gait or pain. I have more range of motion in these shoes. I look and feel like a normal person. I was in tears… I did not think I could ever take a step without pain, much less without a limp or a gait that was pronounced. I can push the accelerator on the car with my shoes on. I wasn’t able to do that before. The deep cushion on the bottom of the shoes alleviates pain and pressure when I need more range of motion. I asked God for healing and He gave me a pair of shoes, Nike Motiva

Ultraboosts w/o the overengineering. Better imo. I'm 6'3/205 lbs with medium arches, wide feet, and around 12.5 shoe size.  I see these shoes basically as a budget ultraboost, a happy medium between the 1.0 and the light. Not as comfortable as the ultraboost 1.0 but more structurally sound. Not as structurally sound as the ultraboost light but more comfortable.  The outsole is not as "cushy" as the ultraboost, thus you experience more ground-feel. However, as someone who is extremely active and on my feet most of the day, this is what you want in the long run. Overly cushy soles lead to weak feet and are bad for your knees.  So if you want the ultraboost look but without the costly overengineering, then I highly recommend these

Very poor quality. I bought a pair of Adidas SL 72’s in the London store a few weeks ago. Initially loved the look, however they made a strange, squeaky noise which I thought was due to the fact that they were new. The noise wasn’t particularly an issue as I liked the look and feel of them. Fast forward less than a month and the sole on one of them is coming away. Furthermore, one of the stripes on the side is also coming away, so really disappointing quality. What was worse was the attitude of Adidas staff when I tried to get the matter resolved. I was referred to WhatsApp and the response to my complaint was to contact the store. I did this, but the lady on the phone insisted that I return them to the London store. I live a good 2 hours from London and it would cost me the same in the value of the shoes getting there. After I tried explaining this, the woman didn’t listen and clearly couldn’t care less. I am therefore left over 90 pounds out of pocket with a pair of trainers which are less than a month old and falling apart. I am a 51 year old man and all I have done in them is walk; certainly no hard yards to make them fall apart. Disgusting quality and frankly disgusting customer service from a company who clearly couldn’t care less. I shall take my business elsewhere in future!

Great trainer with great discount. I purchased this product as it appealed to me by the colours - mainly my favourite - and was on offer with a nice big % off, so much so that I allowed myself the indulgence of personalisation which was an added feature. The customisation process was easy and accurate and being able to choose emojis too (although a small range) was fun. The ordering and payment process was smooth, email communications too. The product was delivered on time to the store I selected, and packaging was sturdy and undamaged. I would add that I questioned if the outer box that the trainers box was inside was really necessary though, thinking about material waste. The trainers inside were fresh, quality check found no faults and custom personalities printing on the sides was well done and smart. Once tried on the foot fit was a bit tight and a squeeze, however at the end of a day out and about wearing them the comfort prevailed and I was satisfied they would be comfortable going forward. Overall I am very happy with this product. Thanks Adidas!

Too good to be true!. The adidas NY 90 shoes bring a nostalgic, retro-inspired design with a modern twist, making them a standout in the brand’s lineup. Inspired by the street culture and fashion scene of the ‘90s, these sneakers feature a combination of premium leather and suede for a stylish yet durable construction. The upper is complemented by adidas’ signature three-stripes, adding a touch of authenticity and heritage. The cushioned EVA midsole ensures comfort and support, making them suitable for daily wear or casual outings. Interesting daily trainer. Initial impression after a short run: - MIDFOOT: foam is dense exactly like Adios Pro 3’s as expected, good enough grip with the Conti, no slipping and sliding on damp pavement with autumn leaves, but could’ve been grippier - FOREFOOT: feels very close to the ground, which I don’t love - HEEL: a little unstable especially at slower paces - UPPER: really don’t like the tongue, laces are thin and kinda annoying but do stay in place, material doesn’t feel premium, and could use a heel tab as putting them on is a bit fiddly - WIDTH: as someone who has normal width feet, this feels a bit narrow in the middle, with my feet bulging out a little to the side - RIDE: feels a lot better at faster efforts vs easy paces, but kinda miss having some stiffness - COMP: it’s not a comp for the Superblast 2 as initially thought, which still feels more premium and better all around (incl stability and grip) especially for long runs, probably closer to Rebel v4, Novablast 4 or Pegasus Plus - OVERALL: i do still really like it, would be interesting to see how it breaks in over time and looking forward to taking it out on some longer miles
